ops.description: Pre-existing bin/ab holes surfaced in the abwc 0.11.0 round-2/3 review (not introduced by 0.11.0): (5a) create_session POST can succeed while still in process-substitution before created_sid is armed → leak window; (5b) created_sid cleared just before the final exec → an exec failure/signal leaves the session live; (10) outside a recognized Claude session cookiesync requestor falls back to parent PID (persistence defeated) and the slug sanitizer is non-injective (a/b and a?b both → ab-a-b, sharing browser state). All predate abwc 0.11.0. CLOSED INLINE in 0.11.0: the double-release-on-second-signal edge (on_exit now snapshot-and-clears created_sid + on_sig disarms INT/TERM). REMAINING: arm created_sid before the POST returns via a temp marker (5a); handle the exec-failure/signal window (5b); a collision-resistant slug (10, hash suffix).
